content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1160 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • • Administration • • • Executive/Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5662         322-5660              322-5901           322-5441              322-6444

February 6, 1980


Oscar D. Berris
1825 Sandiliff Road
Palm Springs, CA 92262

Dear Mr. Berris:

     Thank you for your letter dated January 18, 1980, seeking
informal advice regarding the conflict of interest provisions of
the Political Reform Act.  The facts as I understand them are as
follows.

     Three members of a city council disqualified themselves
from a governmental decision in which they were financially
interested.  As a result of their disqualification, the council
lacked a quorum.  In order to reestablish a quorum so that official
actions could be taken, one of the previously disqualified members
of the council was allowed to participate in the government
decision affecting his interests.  The selection of this individual
was determined by lot and not on the basis of his having the
least financial interest among the disqualified members.  You
have asked whether this selection process is proper.

     The Commission has provided by formal opinion that if a
board cannot, as a result of board member disqualifications,
obtain a quorum in order to make decisions it is legally required
to make the board may bring back as many disqualified members as
is necessary to establish a quorum.  See Opinion requested by
Matthew L. Hudson, 4 FPPC Opinions 13 (No. 77-007, Feb. 7, 1978).
With respect to the selection process the Commission said the
following:

     Turning to the question of the manner in which the ...
     disqualified member should be chosen, we believe that the
     preferred means for selection is by lot or other means of
     random selection.  However, we do not believe that the
     Political Reform Act prevents the use of other impartial
     and equitable means of selection of the disqualified member.

     Hudson, 4 FPPC Opinions 13,
     17-18 (No. 77-007, Feb. 7, 1978)

Oscar D. Berris
February 6, 1980
Page Two


     Based on the Hudson opinion the city acted properly by
selecting a previously disqualified councilmember on a random
basis. However, neither the Hudson opinion nor any other pro-
vision of the Act would prevent the city from adopting a different
procedure which would attempt to determine which board member
has the least financial interest in the decision.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]

                                        Dwight E. Dickerson
                                        Counsel
                                        Legal Division

DED:plh

January 18, 1980


Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, Ca. 95904

Gentlemen:

Please send me a copy of Government Code Section 87100.

What would be your opinion in the following situation:

A building contractor filed an application for a change in zoning. The XYZ Company is the owner of the land involved. The matter is brought on for consideration by the City Council consisting of five members which includes the Mayor. When the matter first came on for a hearing the Mayor disqualified himself because he was the Auditor employed by the XYZ Company. Two other Councilmen also disqualified themselves for a conflict of interest. One of the two claimed that he owned his condominium across the street from the proposed site and the other's conflict of interest lied in the fact that a business associate had a proprietary interest in property adjacent to the proposed site.

At the adjourned meeting, instead of assigning the right to vote to the one with the least amount of conflict, they drew lots and the Mayor, with the greatest conflict, won. Up to then the vote was one for the change and one against. The matter was then left to the Mayor who broke the tie by voting in favor.

Your comments would be greatly appreciated.

Sincerely,

C. D. Berris

OSCAR D. BERRIS
1825 Sandcliff Road
Palm Springs, California 92262
